Mullin (Verbascum thapsus) is a biennial herb, belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly found in Europe and Asia. The health benefits of Mullin include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Mullin include verbascoside, isoverbascoside, and ursolic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mullin include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, as well as skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
